title: Sleep And Brain Function
definition: The naturally recurring state of reduced consciousness and sensory activity essential for health, with implications for brain function, cognition, immune system, emotional regulation, and waste clearance.

difficulty_levels.beginner: A period when your body restores energy, consolidates memories, and clears waste from the brain, essential for good health.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A state of reduced consciousness and sensory activity that is crucial for physical restoration, memory consolidation, and waste clearance from the brain.
difficulty_levels.expert: A complex physiological process involving reduced consciousness and sensory activity, essential for brain function, cognition, immune system regulation, emotional regulation, and waste clearance.

extra_fields.insight: The intricate relationship between sleep and brain function, shedding light on the restorative and regulatory roles of sleep.
